title: The Meaning Of Higher Real Yields
gist: Long-term US TIPS yields have broken above their post-2008 highs even as nominal 20- and 30-year treasury yields stay within their three-year ranges, signaling a genuine rise in the real cost of capital in the US that is not explained by capital flight.

insights.claim: Yields on long-term US treasury inflation-protected securities have broken above their post-2008-crisis highs, even though nominal 20-year and 30-year treasury yields remain within their ranges of the past three years.
insights.speaker: Tan Kai Xian
insights.so_what: The divergence isolates the real (inflation-stripped) component as the driver, so investors should watch TIPS rather than nominal yields to gauge tightening in the true cost of capital.
insights.quote: the yields on long-term US treasury inflation-protected securities have broken above their post-2008-crisis highs

insights.claim: The rising real cost of capital in the US is not being driven by capital flowing out of the country, because the US dollar has appreciated rather than fallen as real yields have risen in recent months.
insights.speaker: Tan Kai Xian
insights.so_what: Ruling out capital flight points investors toward domestic demand-for-capital or supply explanations, and a firm dollar alongside higher real yields pressures rate-sensitive and dollar-funded assets.
insights.quote: The US dollar has appreciated—not fallen—as real yields have risen over recent months.
insights.locator: First, it is safe to say
